symbols: bound and tortured figure — the cost of faithfulness unto death
cherubs above — heaven witnessing and receiving the martyr's sacrifice
woman tending the martyr — the faithful support amid persecution
classical columns — the Roman imperial setting of early Christian martyrdom
description: A dramatic Baroque composition showing the martyrdom of a saint. At center, a semi-nude male figure is bound and subjected to torture, while a woman kneels beside him tending to his wounds or offering comfort. Executioners with muscular bodies stand around wielding instruments. A figure in a red robe gestures from the right. Above, cherubs float in clouds amid a classical architectural setting with columns. A fallen figure lies in the lower foreground. The painting is dark and dramatic with strong chiaroscuro and rich warm tones.
